label: Trino

bindings.name: ingress-setup
bindings._description: Writes the canonical ingress shape every KDPS chart accepts. The `hosts[]` entry uses the rich object form `{host, paths[]}` so OpenMetadata- and Trino-style charts work out of the box; the Superset chart's template was made shape-tolerant (accepts string OR object) in chart >= 0.12.25.

bindings.name: ingress-nginx-tuning
bindings._description: Backslash-escaped dots in the leaf key — the UI binding engine (bindings.ts:pathToParts) treats backslash-dot as a literal dot so the rendered values look like `ingress.annotations: { "nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffer-size": "..." }`.

bindings.name: hadoop-group-mapping-toggle
bindings._description: Sets hadoopConf.groupMapping.enabled — gates the init-container that splices LdapGroupsMapping into core-site.xml (chart >= 1.43.0).

bindings.name: hadoop-group-mapping-ldap-props
bindings._description: Map every Hadoop LdapGroupsMapping form field to its helm path. All paths under hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.* are read by the trino chart's init-hadoop-conf-overlay init container.

form.name: nameOverride
form.label: Nom logique (nameOverride)
form.help: Sera concaténé au releaseName pour former le fullname (ex: <release>-clemlab-trino)

fields.name: accessControl.type
fields.label: Enable Access Control
fields.help: Activer l'intégration avec Apache Ranger pour le contrôle d'accès.

fields.name: rangerAdminUrl
fields.label: Ranger admin
fields.help: The Ranger of the selected Platform Context (right panel) whose policies Trino's plugin enforces. Resolved by KDPS from the context.

fields.name: monitoring.discovery
fields.label: Discover monitoring stack
fields.help: Auto-fill monitoring namespace and release from installed kube-prometheus-stack.

fields.name: monitoring.release
fields.label: Monitoring release (Helm)
fields.help: Utilisé pour construire l'URL du Prometheus interne.

fields.name: internalSharedSecret
fields.label: Shared secret interne
fields.help: Valeur utilisée pour internal-communication.shared-secret afin de sécuriser les communications internes Trino.

fields.name: webUi.enabled
fields.label: Activer l'interface Web
fields.help: Pilote web-ui.enabled dans Trino (UI toujours disponible par défaut).

fields.name: ingress.enabled
fields.label: Enable Ingress
fields.help: Expose the service through a Kubernetes Ingress. Required when an authentication profile (OIDC / LDAP-over-SSL) is selected in Step 1 — OAuth callbacks need a public hostname and HTTPS.

fields.name: ingress.ingressClassName
fields.label: Ingress class
fields.help: Ingress class name (e.g. 'nginx', 'openshift-default'). Leave empty for the default controller.

fields.name: ingress.host
fields.label: Hostname (DNS)
fields.help: Public DNS name the ingress controller will route. e.g. service.example.com.

fields.name: ingress.path
fields.label: HTTP path
fields.help: Base path for the Ingress (usually '/').

fields.name: ingress.tlsMode
fields.label: TLS mode
fields.help: Pick how this service gets its TLS leaf. Recommended path is **Sign via cert-manager** when cert-manager is installed in the cluster (auto-renewal, standard K8s pattern). Fall back to **Sign with Company CA** (if you've uploaded one to the PKI registry) or **Sign with Ambari Internal CA** (testing only, browsers will warn). Options whose backing system isn't installed are hidden automatically. TLS is mandatory when an auth profile is selected in Step 1.

options.label: Source from external Secret store (Vault, ESO)
None (HTTP only — not allowed with auth)

fields.name: ingress.tlsSecret
fields.label: Existing TLS Secret name
fields.help: Name of a kubernetes.io/tls Secret already present in the release namespace. The chart references it via ingress.tls[].secretName.

fields.name: ingress.tlsUpload.cert
fields.label: TLS certificate (PEM, leaf + chain)
fields.help: Paste the full chain (leaf first). Saved as tls.crt in the new Secret.

fields.name: ingress.tlsUpload.key
fields.label: TLS private key (PEM)
fields.help: Paste the private key matching the certificate above. Saved as tls.key.

fields.name: ingress.tlsUpload.secretName
fields.label: Target Secret name
fields.help: Name of the kubernetes.io/tls Secret the view will create from the uploaded cert/key. Leave blank to use the default `<releaseName>-ingress-tls` (computed server-side at deploy time).

fields.name: ingress.tlsSelfSign.caName
fields.label: Company CA
fields.help: Pick a Company Issuing CA from the PKI registry. The view mints a leaf TLS cert signed by this CA before installing the chart. CAs are stored as Kubernetes Secrets in the `ambari-pki` namespace labeled `ambari.clemlab.com/resource-type=issuing-ca`. Upload CAs via Cluster security → Certificate Authorities.

fields.name: ingress.tlsSelfSign.validityDays
fields.label: Leaf validity (days)
fields.help: How long the minted leaf certificate is valid. Capped server-side at 825 (CA/Browser Forum baseline).

fields.name: ingress.certManager.issuerName
fields.label: ClusterIssuer
fields.help: Pick a cert-manager ClusterIssuer or Issuer. The view writes a Certificate resource pre-install; cert-manager mints the Secret and auto-renews it before expiry.

fields.name: ingress.certManager.validityDays
fields.label: Leaf validity (days)
fields.help: Validity in days. Translated to durationHours on the Certificate resource. cert-manager renews at duration/6 before expiry.

fields.name: ingress.externalSecret.secretStoreName
fields.label: SecretStore
fields.help: Pick an external-secrets SecretStore or ClusterSecretStore. Typically Vault-backed for TLS leaves (PKI mount).

fields.name: ingress.externalSecret.remoteKey
fields.label: Remote key / path
fields.help: Path/key in the upstream store. For Vault PKI: 'pki/issue/<role>'. For Vault KV: 'secret/data/tls/<name>'. For AWS Secrets Manager: the secret name.

fields.name: ingress.externalSecret.refreshInterval
fields.label: Refresh interval
fields.help: ESO refresh cadence. e.g. '1h', '15m', '0' (no periodic refresh).

fields.name: ingressTuning
fields.label: Ingress tuning (nginx)

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBufferSize
fields.label: proxy-buffer-size
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffer-size. Leave blank to use chart default (16k under OIDC/OAuth, otherwise nginx default 4k/8k). Bump if you see 502 + 'upstream sent too big header' on OAuth callbacks.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBuffersNumber
fields.label: proxy-buffers-number
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-buffers-number. Total response buffers = number × size.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBusyBuffersSize
fields.label: proxy-busy-buffers-size
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-busy-buffers-size. Should be ≥ proxy-buffer-size and ≤ total buffer capacity.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyBodySize
fields.label: proxy-body-size
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size. Maximum request body the proxy accepts (e.g. '64m'). Bump for large uploads / file imports.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyReadTimeout
fields.label: proxy-read-timeout (seconds)
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout. Bump for long-running queries (Trino, Superset). Default 60.

fields.name: ingress.tuning.proxyConnectTimeout
fields.label: proxy-connect-timeout (seconds)
fields.help: nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-connect-timeout. Time the proxy waits when opening a connection to the backend pod.

Advanced — usually leave blank. nginx ingress annotations (proxy buffers / timeouts). Chart-side defaults are sensible for the common case; expand if you need to override (large headers on OAuth, long-running queries, big uploads).

fields.name: tls.https.enabled
fields.label: Activer HTTPS interne
fields.help: Active le serveur HTTPS de Trino. Génère un keystore signé par l'autorité interne si demandé.

fields.name: tls.https.autoGenerate
fields.label: Auto-générer le keystore
fields.help: Si activé, Ambari génère un certificat signé par la CA interne et un Secret Kubernetes.

fields.name: tls.https.secretName
fields.label: Secret keystore (optionnel)
fields.help: Nom du Secret qui contiendra le keystore. Vide → <release>-https.

fields.name: tls.https.keystoreKey
fields.label: Clé du keystore dans le Secret

fields.name: tls.https.password
fields.label: Mot de passe keystore
fields.help: Laisser vide pour génération aléatoire.

fields.name: tls.https.keystorePath
fields.label: Chemin utilisé par Trino
fields.help: Valeur injectée dans server.config.https.keystore.path.

fields.name: tls.https.keystoreType
fields.label: Format du keystore (JKS/PKCS12/PEM)
fields.help: Choisissez le format de stockage du certificat serveur. PEM crée tls.crt/tls.key/ca.crt.

fields.name: hiveCatalog.enabled
fields.label: Enable Hive catalog
fields.help: When ON, KDPS creates a Trino 'hive' catalog connected to the Hive Metastore of the selected Platform Context (right panel).

fields.name: hiveCatalog.metastoreUri
fields.label: Hive Metastore URI
fields.help: Delivered by KDPS from the selected Platform Context. Toggle Override only to point the catalog at a specific metastore (for example an external context without an Ambari to resolve it).

form.name: hadoopGroupMapping
form.label: Mapping de groupes LDAP (Hadoop UGI)
form.help: Active la résolution des groupes Hadoop via un annuaire LDAP/FreeIPA. Indispensable pour que les politiques Ranger basées sur les groupes (banque_*, assurance_*, etc.) s'appliquent aux requêtes Trino (le conteneur Trino ne contient pas de SSSD). Voir HADOOP-LDAP-GROUP-MAPPING.md.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.enabled
fields.label: Activer le mapping LDAP
fields.help: Si activé, un init container fusionne les propriétés LdapGroupsMapping dans core-site.xml au démarrage du pod. Nécessite chart Trino >= 1.43.0.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.url
fields.label: URL LDAP
fields.help: Endpoint LDAP/IPA. Ex: ldap://admin01.dev01.hadoop.clemlab.com:389. Pour LDAPS utiliser ldaps://...:636 et vérifier que le CA est présent dans le truststore Trino.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.bindUser
fields.label: Utilisateur de bind
fields.help: DN du compte de service utilisé pour interroger l'annuaire (IPA cache memberOf en lecture anonyme — un bind authentifié est requis). Ex: uid=hadoop-group-mapping,cn=users,cn=accounts,dc=dev01,dc=hadoop,dc=clemlab,dc=com

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.bindPasswordSecret.name
fields.label: Nom du Secret contenant le mot de passe
fields.help: Nom d'un Kubernetes Secret du namespace cible (à créer préalablement) qui contient le mot de passe du compte de bind. Le chart monte ce Secret sur /etc/hadoop/ldap-bind/password en lecture seule.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.bindPasswordSecret.key
fields.label: Clé dans le Secret
fields.help: Clé du Secret qui contient effectivement le mot de passe (par défaut 'password').

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.baseDn
fields.label: Base DN de recherche
fields.help: Racine de recherche pour les utilisateurs et les groupes. Ex (FreeIPA): cn=accounts,dc=dev01,dc=hadoop,dc=clemlab,dc=com

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.userSearchFilter
fields.label: Filtre utilisateur (avancé)
fields.help: {0} est substitué par le nom de l'utilisateur. Ne pas modifier sauf annuaire non-IPA.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.groupSearchFilter
fields.label: Filtre groupe (avancé)
fields.help: Filtre LDAP pour énumérer les groupes. Compatible avec FreeIPA/RHIDM par défaut.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.memberAttr
fields.label: Attribut membre (avancé)
fields.help: Attribut LDAP qui contient les DN des membres du groupe. FreeIPA = 'member'.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.cacheSeconds
fields.label: Cache positif (secondes, avancé)
fields.help: Durée de mise en cache des résolutions de groupes côté Hadoop client.

fields.name: hadoopConf.groupMapping.ldap.negativeCacheSeconds
fields.label: Cache négatif (secondes, avancé)
fields.help: Durée de mise en cache des utilisateurs sans groupe (évite de marteler LDAP).

description: Distributed SQL query engine for big data. Run ANSI SQL queries across Hive, S3, RDBMS and other sources at scale.
